course.name: Operating Systems
course.studentLevel: Upper-division CS majors with strong C and systems background
course.assessmentFormat: GitHub Classroom lab and in-class activities.

lecture.summary: Cover virtual address space, page table translation, TLB locality, page faults.
lecture.openingHook: Open with a concrete problem that makes Virtual Memory and Paging necessary rather than abstract.
objectives: Explain the core idea behind Virtual Memory and Paging.
Apply virtual address space to a practical computing scenario.
Evaluate the main tradeoffs and failure modes around Virtual Memory and Paging.
caseStudies: TLB misses during a memory-intensive workload
activities: classify workloads as locality-friendly or locality-hostile
takeaways: Explain the core idea behind Virtual Memory and Paging.
Apply virtual address space to a practical computing scenario.
Evaluate the main tradeoffs and failure modes around Virtual Memory and Paging.
discussionQuestions: When is a larger page size a net win?
How should an OS respond when a process begins to thrash?
Which part of paging is most visible to an application developer?

sections.title: Why Virtual Memory Exists
sections.overview: Explain why virtual memory exists and connect it to virtual address space.
points: Define the core idea behind why virtual memory exists.
Show one concrete example that makes why virtual memory exists operational.
Link why virtual memory exists back to the lecture’s main tradeoffs.
activities: Ask students to explain why virtual memory exists in their own words.

sections.title: Page Translation And The TLB
sections.overview: Explain page translation and the tlb and connect it to page table translation.
points: Define the core idea behind page translation and the tlb.
Show one concrete example that makes page translation and the tlb operational.
Link page translation and the tlb back to the lecture’s main tradeoffs.
activities: Ask students to explain page translation and the tlb in their own words.

sections.title: Page Faults And Thrashing
sections.overview: Explain page faults and thrashing and connect it to TLB locality.
points: Define the core idea behind page faults and thrashing.
Show one concrete example that makes page faults and thrashing operational.
Link page faults and thrashing back to the lecture’s main tradeoffs.
activities: Ask students to explain page faults and thrashing in their own words.
